title: LAB Agarose Gel Electrophoresis Buffer Recipe
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1t3h1","text":"A recipe to make lithium acetate / borate agarose gel electrophoresis buffer. This buffer has a lower ionic strength than TAE, but maintains its ability to resolve both small and large bands.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"adsnd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4j7a4","text":"Additionally, it is MUCH less expensive, and if you are short on time (like in a lab course!) you can run the gel at a higher voltage.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54vbr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4d0ed","text":"From https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0011318 via https://bitesizebio.com/25078/faster-even-cooler-dna-gels/","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":5,"length":73},{"key":1,"offset":83,"length":58}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"425039CB012D11EDBE9C0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0011318"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"45A7A18A012D11EDBE9C0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://bitesizebio.com/25078/faster-even-cooler-dna-gels"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"erfo3","text":"Use good laboratory practices for measuring weights and volumes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"egin","text":"Lithium acetate: May cause eye and skin irritation. May cause respiratory and digestive tract irritation. The toxicological properties of this material have not been fully investigated.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"58isi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1bnhk","text":"Boric acid: May damage fertility. May damage the unborn child.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mob9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9h5vr","text":"Wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE), including a lab coat, nitrile gloves and safety glasses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"beab5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Weigh out 25.5 g Lithium Acetate Dihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #L4158 Make sure it's the dihydrate, not anhydrous lithium acetate! If you're using anhydrous, weigh out 16.5 g instead.
Weigh out 15.5 g Boric acid Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP1681 .
Mix the lithium acetate and boric acid with deionized water to a final volume of 1 L
Pipette a small amount onto a Paper pH Strips Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #13-640-508 . Make sure the pH is between 6.5 and 7.0.
Dilute 40 mL of 25X stock solution with 960 mL deionized H2O. Mix well.
creator: Brian Teague
